issues.reason: The west blue-door reward pack is not structurally protected by the blue door. After opening only the central yellow door at [7,10], the player can reach the upper connector around [5,3] and enter the upper-west rewards, bypassing the blue door at [3,7]. This undermines the intended blue-door compensation route.
issues.required_change: Re-wall or gate the upper connector around [5,3]/[5,1], or relocate the west reward pack so [1,5], [3,5], [2,1], and [4,1] are only reachable after the blue door or an equivalent blue-cost/combat gate.

issues.reason: The central route economically dominates the east route: it uses one yellow door, refunds a yellow key, has comparable reward value, and has the same annotated route length, while the east route uses two yellow doors and is annotated for stronger combat/special tax with only equivalent compensation.
issues.required_change: Either reduce the east route tax, add clearly stronger east-route compensation or shortcut value, or increase central-route cost/pressure so the two candidate routes create a real tradeoff.

required_changes: Rework west reward protection so the blue door at [3,7] actually controls the high-value west reward pack or an equivalent gate does.
Rebalance the central-versus-east route tax/reward relationship so the central one-yellow route does not strictly dominate the two-yellow east route.
summary: Tracked quotas exactly match the floor contract and remaining whole-tower budget, the entrance free region exposes only one starter yellow key, and no enemy/tool tiles are placed. The floor fails economy review because blue-door compensation can be bypassed and the east route is overtaxed relative to the central route.
